text.title: go-http-handlers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-http-handlers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using gin.Default() and then adding r.Use(middleware.Logger()) again.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: gin.Default() already registers Logger and Recovery. Adding Logger a second time doubles every log entry and makes the middleware stack invisible to readers. Use gin.New() with explicit middleware registration.

failure-mode.description: Creating context.Background() inside a handler to pass to a service or DB call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: context.Background() is never cancelled and has no deadline. The downstream call outlives the request, keeps connections open, and prevents graceful shutdown from completing. Always use c.Request.Context().

failure-mode.description: Spawning go func() inside a handler that captures c.Request.Context().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When the handler returns, the context is cancelled. The goroutine's next DB or HTTP call returns context.Canceled. Without a recover() inside the goroutine, the panic kills the process. Use a derived context with explicit timeout from context.Background() for fire-and-forget work.

failure-mode.description: Returning raw gin.H{"error": err.Error()} which leaks struct field names and binding tag details.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: gin's validator returns messages like "Field validation for 'Email' failed on the 'email' tag". These expose internal model field names and validation rules to external clients. Wrap in a translator or return a generic error message.

failure-mode.description: CORS configured with AllowOrigins: ["*"] and AllowCredentials: true.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This combination is invalid per the CORS spec. Modern browsers reject credentialed cross-origin requests when the response contains a wildcard origin. Specify origins explicitly.

failure-mode.description: JWT signing key shorter than 32 bytes or hardcoded as a string literal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Short HS256 keys are brute-forceable. Hardcoded keys are committed to source control. Use a minimum 32-byte key loaded from an environment variable or secrets manager, or switch to RS256 with a managed key pair.

failure-mode.description: Graceful shutdown: calling srv.Shutdown(ctx) but not waiting for server.close before exiting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: srv.Shutdown is asynchronous relative to the process exit. Without waiting for the server.close callback, the process exits mid-request. Use the server.Run wrapper that blocks until Shutdown completes.
